  • Overview
  • Tree
  • Index
  • Search
  • Help

    All Classes and Interfaces

    Class
    Description
    AddCommand
    The AddCommand allows the user to add a bead to a position, only if the move is valid.
    AudioController
     
    Bead
     
    BeadColour
    A enumerator which describes a bead colour.
    BeadController
     
    BeadView
     
    Board
     
    BoardController
     
    BoardView
    BoardView initializes a fixed visual board and provides a way to draw these graphics to a Panel.
    ButtonController
    ButtonController manages a buttons interactions, updates, drawing, and user input handling.
    ButtonView
    ButtonView initializes the visual aspects of a button and provides a way to draw these graphics to a Panel and edit the state of a graphic using the setIndex method.
    ClearCommand
    The ClearCommand clears the current board.
    Command
    A Command is a way the user can interact with the program using string in a command line.
    Controllable
    A Controllable class is a class which need to be updated every game loop.
    DebugCommand
    The DebugCommand displays various debug information to the console.
    DrawBoardCommand
    The DrawBoardCommand shows a console drawing of the board.
    Frame
    A Frame creates a new JFrame where the contents of a Panel can be displayed.
    Game
    The Game object manages the core logic of the game.
    GameMode
    A GameMode allows a program to be run in various ways.
    GameState
    GameState allows the game to gracefully switch between various modes.
    GetMoveCommand
    The GetMoveCommand allows the user to have the program propose a move.
    HelpCommand
    The HelpCommand lists all available Command's and their descriptions.
    Interactable
     
    MenuController
    MenuController manages the menu state interactions, updates, drawing, and user input handling.
    MenuView
    MenuView initializes a fixed visual overlay and provides a way to draw these graphics to a Panel.
    MouseInputs
    MouseInputs listens to various user inputs which occur in a designated Panel.
    NormalMode
    NormalMode is a visually interactive GameMode.
    OverlayController
    OverlayController manages the in game overlay interactions, updates, drawing, and user input handling.
    OverlayView
    OverlayView initializes a non-fixed visual overlay and provides a way to draw these graphics to a Panel.
    Panel
    A Panel which acts as the main rendering and input handler for a Game
    Peg
     
    PegView
     
    PlayingController
    PlayingController manages the play state interactions, updates, drawing, and user input handling.
    PlayingView
    PlayingView initializes fixed in-game visuals and provides a way to draw these graphics to a Panel.
    QuitCommand
    The QuitCommand gracefully quits the program.
    RemoveCommand
    The RemoveCommand allows the user to remove a bead from a specified location, if it exists.
    ScoreFour
    This program simulates a Score Four game.
    ShowBoardCommand
    The ShowBoardCommand shows a text representation of the board.
    StartGUICommand
    The StartGUICommand allows the user to start a GUI instance, if one doesn't already exist.
    StopGUICommand
    The StopGUICommand allows the user to stop the current GUI instance, if one exists.
    TestMode
    TestMode is a GameMode which allows the user to control the program through commands.
    Viewable
    Classes which render images to a Panel are considered Viewable.
